author: Evan Lezar
message: Remove libnvidia-container0 dependency
patch:
commit 18309e4548063537c8fc38c438a75761726f7a7e
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 25 13:35:03 2023 +0200

    Remove libnvidia-container0 dependency
    
    This change removes the dependency on libnvidia-container0
    on ubuntu-based arm64 systems. The library is required to support
    the legacy mode on Tegra-based systems. This has not been the
    default since the 1.10.0 release of the NVIDIA Container Toolkit.
    
    If the legacy mode is required, the libnvidia-container0 packages
    can be installed separately from the libnvidia-container-tools
    pacakge.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 Makefile                               | 2 +-
 mk/Dockerfile.ubuntu                   | 2 --
 mk/docker.mk                           | 4 ----
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 2 +-
 pkg/deb/control                        | 2 +-
 pkg/deb/prepare                        | 3 +--
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 2 +-
 7 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 00d561e1..5c1d5f6b 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -341,7 +341,7 @@ deb: prefix:=/usr
 deb: libdir:=/usr/lib/@DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH@
 deb: install
 	$(CP) -T $(PKG_DIR)/deb $(DESTDIR)/debian
-	cd $(DESTDIR) && debuild -eDISTRIB -eSECTION -eLIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY --dpkg-buildpackage-hook='debian/prepare %v' -a$(ARCH) -us -uc -B
+	cd $(DESTDIR) && debuild -eDISTRIB -eSECTION --dpkg-buildpackage-hook='debian/prepare %v' -a$(ARCH) -us -uc -B
 	cd $(DESTDIR) && (yes | debuild clean || yes | debuild -- clean)
 
 rpm: DESTDIR:=$(DIST_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME)_$(VERSION)_$(ARCH)
diff --git a/mk/Dockerfile.ubuntu b/mk/Dockerfile.ubuntu
index d5b0f06e..84a207d3 100644
--- a/mk/Dockerfile.ubuntu
+++ b/mk/Dockerfile.ubuntu
@@ -50,8 +50,6 @@ ENV WITH_SECCOMP=${WITH_SECCOMP}
 
 ARG REVISION
 ENV REVISION=${REVISION}
-ARG LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY
-ENV LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY ${LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY}
 
 RUN make distclean && make -j"$(nproc)"
 
diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index 6fc01a27..e8493a89 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -123,9 +123,6 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --debian%: OS := debian
 --amazonlinux%: OS := amazonlinux
 
-# For the ubuntu18.04 arm64 target we add a dependency on libnvidia-container0 to ensure that libnvidia-container-tools also supports Jetson devices
---ubuntu18.04-arm64: LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY = libnvidia-container0 (= 0.9.0~beta.1) | libnvidia-container0 (>= 0.10.0+jetpack)
-
 # private centos target with overrides
 --centos%: OS := centos
 --centos%: WITH_TIRPC = yes
@@ -164,7 +161,6 @@ docker-build-%: $(ARTIFACTS_DIR)
 	    --build-arg CFLAGS="$(CFLAGS)" \
 	    --build-arg LDLIBS="$(LDLIBS)" \
 	    --build-arg REVISION="$(REVISION)" \
-	    --build-arg LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY="$(LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY)" \
 	    $(EXTRA_BUILD_ARGS) \
 	    --tag $(BUILDIMAGE) \
 	    --file $(DOCKERFILE) .
diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 87e64501..f555be25 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.14.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
-  * Bump version to 1.14.0-rc.1
+  * Remove linvidia-container0 dependency on Ubuntu-based arm64 platforms
 
  -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Mon, 24 Apr 2023 17:30:18 +0200
 
diff --git a/pkg/deb/control b/pkg/deb/control
index 135cd3f6..8b4b4023 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/control
+++ b/pkg/deb/control
@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@ Package: libnvidia-container-tools
 Section: @SECTION@utils
 Priority: optional
 Architecture: any
-Depends: @LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY@, ${shlibs:Depends}, ${misc:Depends}
+Depends: ${shlibs:Depends}, ${misc:Depends}
 Description: NVIDIA container runtime library (command-line tools)
  The nvidia-container library provides an interface to configure GNU/Linux
  containers leveraging NVIDIA hardware. The implementation relies on several
diff --git a/pkg/deb/prepare b/pkg/deb/prepare
index e1180857..92ec725a 100755
--- a/pkg/deb/prepare
+++ b/pkg/deb/prepare
@@ -20,12 +20,11 @@ find . -type d -name '*@DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH@*' | sed "p;s;@DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH@;$D
 find . -type f -name '*@MAJOR@*' | sed "p;s;@MAJOR@;$MAJOR;" | \
   xargs -r -n2 sh -c 'rm -f $1 && mv -T $0 $1'
 
-grep -rIlE '@(SECTION|VERSION|MAJOR|DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH|LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY|VERSION_TAG)@' . | \
+grep -rIlE '@(SECTION|VERSION|MAJOR|DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH|VERSION_TAG)@' . | \
   xargs -r sed -i "s;@SECTION@;${SECTION:+$SECTION/};
                    s;@VERSION@;$VERSION;
                    s;@MAJOR@;$MAJOR;
                    s;@DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH@;$DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH;
-                   s;@LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY@;${LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY:+${LIBNVIDIA_CONTAINER0_DEPENDENCY}, };
                    s;@VERSION_TAG@;${VERSION_TAG:-${VERSION}};"
 
 if [ -n "$DISTRIB" ]; then
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index b89124dc..08ff85bc 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 
 %changelog
 * Mon Apr 24 2023 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.14.0-rc.1-1
-- Bump version to 1.14.0-rc.1
+- Remove linvidia-container0 dependency on Ubuntu-based arm64 platforms
 
 * Thu Apr 13 2023 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.13.1-1
 - Bump version to 1.13.1

author: Evan Lezar
message: Remove fedora make targets
patch:
commit 559800d3873007150f47b5613b7debd9a68038c6
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 22 19:53:12 2023 +0200

    Remove fedora make targets
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/Dockerfile.fedora | 63 ----------------------------------------------------
 mk/docker.mk         | 16 ++-----------
 2 files changed, 2 insertions(+), 77 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mk/Dockerfile.fedora b/mk/Dockerfile.fedora
deleted file mode 100644
index c5e9e47a..00000000
--- a/mk/Dockerfile.fedora
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,63 +0,0 @@
-ARG BASEIMAGE
-FROM ${BASEIMAGE}
-
-SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
-
-RUN yum install -y \
-        bzip2 \
-        createrepo \
-        elfutils-libelf-devel \
-        bmake \
-        gcc \
-        git \
-        libcap-devel \
-        libseccomp-devel \
-        m4 \
-        make \
-        redhat-lsb-core \
-        libtirpc-devel \
-        rpcgen \
-        rpm-build \
-        rpm-sign \
-        rpmlint \
-        which && \
-    rm -rf /var/cache/yum/*
-
-ARG OS_ARCH
-ARG GOLANG_VERSION
-ENV OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH}
-RUN OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH/x86_64/amd64} && OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH/aarch64/arm64} && \
-    curl https://storage.googleapis.com/golang/go${GOLANG_VERSION}.linux-${OS_ARCH}.tar.gz \
-    | tar -C /usr/local -xz
-ENV GOPATH /go
-ENV PATH $GOPATH/bin:/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH
-
-ARG WITH_NVCGO=no
-ARG WITH_LIBELF=no
-ARG WITH_TIRPC=no
-ARG WITH_SECCOMP=yes
-ENV WITH_NVCGO=${WITH_NVCGO}
-ENV WITH_LIBELF=${WITH_LIBELF}
-ENV WITH_TIRPC=${WITH_TIRPC}
-ENV WITH_SECCOMP=${WITH_SECCOMP}
-
-WORKDIR /tmp/libnvidia-container
-COPY . .
-
-ARG CFLAGS
-ARG LDLIBS
-ENV CFLAGS=${CFLAGS}
-ENV LDLIBS=${LDLIBS}
-
-ARG REVISION
-ENV REVISION=${REVISION}
-RUN make distclean && make -j"$(nproc)"
-
-# Use the revision as the package version for the time being
-ENV PKG_NAME libnvidia-container
-ENV PKG_VERS ${REVISION}
-ENV DIST_DIR=/tmp/${PKG_NAME}-${PKG_VERS}
-RUN mkdir -p $DIST_DIR /dist
-CMD make dist && \
-    make rpm && \
-    mv /tmp/${PKG_NAME}-${PKG_VERS}/*.rpm /dist;
diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index 335d2729..6fc01a27 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -33,10 +33,10 @@ REVISION 	 ?= $(shell git rev-parse HEAD)
 
 # Supported OSs by architecture
 AMD64_TARGETS := ubuntu20.04 ubuntu18.04 ubuntu16.04 debian10 debian9
-X86_64_TARGETS := fedora35 centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8 amazonlinux2 opensuse-leap15.1
+X86_64_TARGETS := centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8 amazonlinux2 opensuse-leap15.1
 PPC64LE_TARGETS := ubuntu18.04 ubuntu16.04 centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8
 ARM64_TARGETS := ubuntu18.04
-AARCH64_TARGETS := fedora35 centos8 rhel8 amazonlinux2
+AARCH64_TARGETS := centos8 rhel8 amazonlinux2
 
 # Define top-level build targets
 docker%: SHELL:=/bin/bash
@@ -131,18 +131,6 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --centos%: WITH_TIRPC = yes
 --centos8%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
 
-# private fedora target with overrides
---fedora%: OS := fedora
---fedora%: CFLAGS := -I/usr/include/tirpc
---fedora%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
-# The fedora(35) base image has very slow performance when building aarch64 packages.
-# Since our primary concern here is glibc versions, we use the older glibc version available in centos8.
---fedora35%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
---fedora35%: OS := centos
-# We need to specify this version to ensure that the correct packages are installed in the centos8 build image
---fedora35%: VERSION := 8
---fedora35%: ARTIFACTS_DIR = $(DIST_DIR)/fedora35/$(ARCH)
-
 # private opensuse-leap target with overrides
 --opensuse-leap%: OS := opensuse-leap
 --opensuse-leap%: BASEIMAGE = opensuse/leap:$(VERSION)

message: Fix building with WITH_TIRPC=yes

Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 22 17:39:43 2023 +0200

author: Carlos Eduardo Arango Gutierrez
message: Add blossom-ci github action
patch:
commit 7e45b64859112aad54d632684d2f92673df090c8
Author: Carlos Eduardo Arango Gutierrez <eduardoa@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 17 15:59:51 2023 +0100

    Add blossom-ci github action
    
    Signed-off-by: Carlos Eduardo Arango Gutierrez <eduardoa@nvidia.com>
---
 .github/workflows/blossom-ci.yaml | 113 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 113 insertions(+)

diff --git a/.github/workflows/blossom-ci.yaml b/.github/workflows/blossom-ci.yaml
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..0ffe8b8e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/workflows/blossom-ci.yaml
@@ -0,0 +1,113 @@
+# Copyright (c) 2020-2023, NVIDIA CORPORATION.
+#
+# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+# You may obtain a copy of the License at
+#
+#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+#
+# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+# limitations under the License.
+
+# A workflow to trigger ci on hybrid infra (github + self hosted runner)
+name: Blossom-CI
+on:
+  issue_comment:
+    types: [created]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+      inputs:
+          platform:
+            description: 'runs-on argument'     
+            required: false
+          args:
+            description: 'argument'     
+            required: false
+jobs:
+  Authorization:
+    name: Authorization
+    runs-on: blossom 
+    outputs:
+      args: ${{ env.args }}
+     
+    # This job only runs for pull request comments
+    if: |
+         contains( '\
+         anstockatnv,\
+         rohitrajani2018,\
+         cdesiniotis,\
+         shivamerla,\
+         ArangoGutierrez,\
+         elezar,\
+         klueska,\
+         zvonkok,\
+         ', format('{0},', github.actor)) && 
+         github.event.comment.body == '/blossom-ci'  
+    steps:
+      - name: Check if comment is issued by authorized person
+        run: blossom-ci
+        env:
+          OPERATION: 'AUTH'
+          REPO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          REPO_KEY_DATA: ${{ secrets.BLOSSOM_KEY }}
+      
+  Vulnerability-scan:
+    name: Vulnerability scan
+    needs: [Authorization]
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout code
+        uses: actions/checkout@v2
+        with:
+          repository: ${{ fromJson(needs.Authorization.outputs.args).repo }}
+          ref: ${{ fromJson(needs.Authorization.outputs.args).ref }}
+          lfs: 'true'
+      
+      # repo specific steps 
+      #- name: Setup java
+      #  uses: actions/setup-java@v1
+      #  with:
+      #    java-version: 1.8
+      
+      # add blackduck properties https://synopsys.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/INTDOCS/pages/631308372/Methods+for+Configuring+Analysis#Using-a-configuration-file
+      #- name: Setup blackduck properties
+      #  run: |
+      #       PROJECTS=$(mvn -am dependency:tree | grep maven-dependency-plugin | awk '{ out="com.nvidia:"$(NF-1);print out }' | grep rapids | xargs | sed -e 's/ /,/g')
+      #       echo detect.maven.build.command="-pl=$PROJECTS -am" >> application.properties
+      #       echo detect.maven.included.scopes=compile >> application.properties
+          
+      - name: Run blossom action
+        uses: NVIDIA/blossom-action@main
+        env:
+          REPO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          REPO_KEY_DATA: ${{ secrets.BLOSSOM_KEY }}
+        with:
+          args1: ${{ fromJson(needs.Authorization.outputs.args).args1 }}
+          args2: ${{ fromJson(needs.Authorization.outputs.args).args2 }}
+          args3: ${{ fromJson(needs.Authorization.outputs.args).args3 }}
+          
+  Job-trigger:
+    name: Start ci job
+    needs: [Vulnerability-scan]
+    runs-on: blossom
+    steps:
+      - name: Start ci job
+        run: blossom-ci
+        env:
+          OPERATION: 'START-CI-JOB'
+          CI_SERVER: ${{ secrets.CI_SERVER }}
+          REPO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+              
+  Upload-Log:
+    name: Upload log
+    runs-on: blossom
+    if : github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
+    steps:
+      - name: Jenkins log for pull request ${{ fromJson(github.event.inputs.args).pr }} (click here)
+        run: blossom-ci
+        env:
+          OPERATION: 'POST-PROCESSING'
+          CI_SERVER: ${{ secrets.CI_SERVER }}
+          REPO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

author: Evan Lezar
message: Add manual trigger of pipelines on MRs
patch:
commit dba2c84a6143d6c0646afa5ca6af5a079fed43a7
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 15 09:23:03 2023 +0200

    Add manual trigger of pipelines on MRs
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 .gitlab-ci.yml | 19 +++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 19 insertions(+)

diff --git a/.gitlab-ci.yml b/.gitlab-ci.yml
index da1cd224..50e29ad3 100644
--- a/.gitlab-ci.yml
+++ b/.gitlab-ci.yml
@@ -19,8 +19,17 @@ default:
       command: ["--experimental"]
 
 stages:
+  - trigger
   - package-build
 
+workflow:
+  rules:
+    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == 'merge_request_event'
+    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "web"
+    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
+    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^release-.*$/
+    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG && $CI_COMMIT_TAG != ""
+
 .main-or-manual:
   rules:
     - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
@@ -28,6 +37,16 @@ stages:
     - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "schedule"
       when: manual
 
+trigger-pipeline:
+  stage: trigger
+  script:
+    - echo "starting pipeline"
+  rules:
+    - !reference [.main-or-manual, rules]
+    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == 'merge_request_event'
+      when: manual
+    - when: always
+
 # Define the package build helpers
 .multi-arch-build:
   before_script:

author: Evan Lezar
message: Fix segfault when detecting firmware on WSL2
patch:
commit c50607646030a9a86621719d06958e016636fe60
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Mar 14 09:24:20 2023 +0200

    Fix segfault when detecting firmware on WSL2
    
    This change initializes the `glob_t` structure to prevent a segfault
    when freeing this on platforms where this is not initialized.
    
    This includes WSL2 but may also be triggered in cases where constructing
    the firmware path fails.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 src/nvc_info.c | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/nvc_info.c b/src/nvc_info.c
index 9e27c3c8..85c9a4d1 100644
--- a/src/nvc_info.c
+++ b/src/nvc_info.c
@@ -434,7 +434,7 @@ lookup_firmwares(struct error *err, struct dxcore_context *dxcore, struct nvc_dr
 {
         (void)flags;
 
-        glob_t gl;
+        glob_t gl = {0};
         char glob_path[PATH_MAX];
         char *firmware_path = NULL;
         int rv = -1;

author: Evan Lezar
message: Use - instead of ~ in version string
patch:
commit a6b63db1aa798288cd698e5368a49bff49fa7b51
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 10 16:09:54 2023 +0100

    Use - instead of ~ in version string
    
    This uses - instead of ~ in the version string reported by the
    nvidia-container-cli --verison command.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 Makefile  | 4 ++--
 src/nvc.h | 4 ++--
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 6fb69765..00d561e1 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -119,8 +119,8 @@ ifeq ($(PATCH),)
 $(error Invalid patch version)
 endif
 
-ifneq ($(VERSION_STRING),$(VERSION)$(if $(TAG),~$(TAG),))
-$(error Version not updated correctly: $(VERSION_STRING) != $(VERSION)$(if $(TAG),~$(TAG),))
+ifneq ($(VERSION_STRING),$(VERSION)$(if $(TAG),-$(TAG),))
+$(error Version not updated correctly: $(VERSION_STRING) != $(VERSION)$(if $(TAG),-$(TAG),))
 endif
 
 BIN_NAME    := nvidia-container-cli
diff --git a/src/nvc.h b/src/nvc.h
index 3f742cd3..0a6f8bbd 100644
--- a/src/nvc.h
+++ b/src/nvc.h
@@ -32,9 +32,9 @@ extern "C" {
 // Specify the release tag.
 // For stable releases, this should be defined as empty.
 // For release candidates, this should be defined with the format "rc.1"
-// The version string should also be updated accordingly, using a ~ separator where applicable.
+// The version string should also be updated accordingly, using a - separator where applicable.
 #define NVC_TAG "rc.1"
-#define NVC_VERSION "1.13.0~rc.1"
+#define NVC_VERSION "1.13.0-rc.1"
 
 #define NVC_ARG_MAX 256

message: Add nvcubins.bin to DriverStore components
patch:
commit d3e977ef74ee9c67c580a7c721d3b5caf22e1546
Author: Jakub Bujak <jbujak@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 26 13:59:27 2023 +0000

    Add nvcubins.bin to DriverStore components
    
    This change adds the new nvubins.bin file to the list of files copied from the DriverStore. The file is added as part of an effort to remove the GPU code from the driver library.
    
    Signed-off-by: Jakub Bujak <jbujak@nvidia.com>
---
 src/dxcore.c | 1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

diff --git a/src/dxcore.c b/src/dxcore.c
index 5a17ce1a..b6b83a7b 100644
--- a/src/dxcore.c
+++ b/src/dxcore.c
@@ -18,6 +18,7 @@ static const char * const dxcore_nvidia_driver_store_components[] = {
         "libnvidia-ml.so.1",                /* Core library for nvml */
         "libnvidia-ml_loader.so",           /* Core library for nvml on WSL */
         "nvidia-smi",                       /* nvidia-smi binary*/
+        "nvcubins.bin",                     /* Binary containing GPU code for cuda */
 };

author: Evan Lezar
message: Ignore check for existing files when creating non-empty files
patch:
commit 4838a97de940c5f4152e60086bb709f18e5b27a3
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Jan 18 16:45:17 2023 +0100

    Ignore check for existing files when creating non-empty files
    
    When creating files the check for existence is skipped if any data
    is specified to write to the file. This ensures that new file contents
    are always written. This fixes a bug where, for example, the
    application-profiles file was not updated correctly for multiple GPUs.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 src/utils.c | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/utils.c b/src/utils.c
index 8fe32e4b..8ff6f269 100644
--- a/src/utils.c
+++ b/src/utils.c
@@ -524,7 +524,7 @@ file_create(struct error *err, const char *path, const char *data, uid_t uid, gi
         int rv = -1;
 
         // We check whether the file already exists with the required mode and skip the creation.
-        if (file_mode(err, path, &perm) == 0) {
+        if (data == NULL && file_mode(err, path, &perm) == 0) {
                 if (perm == mode) {
                         log_errf("The path %s alreay exists with the required mode; skipping create", path);
                         return (0);

author: Evan Lezar
message: Skip file creation if it exists
patch:
commit 10293cf6be08494766ff694078c0f0f00094cb34
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Oct 12 10:50:13 2022 +0200

    Skip file creation if it exists
    
    This change skips the creation of a file if it already exists. This may be
    the case, for example, if the folder containing the file on the host is
    mounted as part of the docker command line.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 2 +-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 2 +-
 src/utils.c                            | 8 ++++++++
 3 files changed, 10 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

         int flags = O_NOFOLLOW|O_CREAT;
         int rv = -1;
 
+        // We check whether the file already exists with the required mode and skip the creation.
+        if (file_mode(err, path, &perm) == 0) {
+                if (perm == mode) {
+                        log_errf("The path %s alreay exists with the required mode; skipping create", path);
+                        return (0);
+                }
+        }
+
         if ((p = xstrdup(err, path)) == NULL)
                 return (-1);

author: Evan Lezar
message: Switch to centos:stream8 container for fedora
patch:
commit 4b159ebfdeeae7f1d7c25527c9e17b7ca603e9ce
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Aug 22 16:11:26 2022 +0200

    Switch to centos:stream8 container for fedora
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/docker.mk | 6 ++++++
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)

diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index bbdefaf8..65a1a9a8 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -136,6 +136,12 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --fedora%: OS := fedora
 --fedora%: CFLAGS := -I/usr/include/tirpc
 --fedora%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
+# The fedora(35) base image has very slow performance when building aarch64 packages.
+# Since our primary concern here is glibc versions, we use the older glibc version available in centos8.
+--fedora35%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
+--fedora35%: OS := centos
+# We need to specify this version to ensure that the correct packages are installed in the centos8 build image
+--fedora35%: VERSION := 8
 
 # private opensuse-leap target with overrides
 --opensuse-leap%: OS := opensuse-leap

author: Kevin Klues
message: Add support for fedora35
patch:
commit 1cb3a9474ac9ed27f8242456f041461d68a7fe8f
Author: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 18 10:50:12 2022 +0000

    Add support for fedora35
    
    Signed-off-by: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/Dockerfile.fedora | 63 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 mk/docker.mk         |  9 ++++++--
 2 files changed, 70 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mk/Dockerfile.fedora b/mk/Dockerfile.fedora
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..c5e9e47a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/mk/Dockerfile.fedora
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+ARG BASEIMAGE
+FROM ${BASEIMAGE}
+
+SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
+
+RUN yum install -y \
+        bzip2 \
+        createrepo \
+        elfutils-libelf-devel \
+        bmake \
+        gcc \
+        git \
+        libcap-devel \
+        libseccomp-devel \
+        m4 \
+        make \
+        redhat-lsb-core \
+        libtirpc-devel \
+        rpcgen \
+        rpm-build \
+        rpm-sign \
+        rpmlint \
+        which && \
+    rm -rf /var/cache/yum/*
+
+ARG OS_ARCH
+ARG GOLANG_VERSION
+ENV OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH}
+RUN OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH/x86_64/amd64} && OS_ARCH=${OS_ARCH/aarch64/arm64} && \
+    curl https://storage.googleapis.com/golang/go${GOLANG_VERSION}.linux-${OS_ARCH}.tar.gz \
+    | tar -C /usr/local -xz
+ENV GOPATH /go
+ENV PATH $GOPATH/bin:/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH
+
+ARG WITH_NVCGO=no
+ARG WITH_LIBELF=no
+ARG WITH_TIRPC=no
+ARG WITH_SECCOMP=yes
+ENV WITH_NVCGO=${WITH_NVCGO}
+ENV WITH_LIBELF=${WITH_LIBELF}
+ENV WITH_TIRPC=${WITH_TIRPC}
+ENV WITH_SECCOMP=${WITH_SECCOMP}
+
+WORKDIR /tmp/libnvidia-container
+COPY . .
+
+ARG CFLAGS
+ARG LDLIBS
+ENV CFLAGS=${CFLAGS}
+ENV LDLIBS=${LDLIBS}
+
+ARG REVISION
+ENV REVISION=${REVISION}
+RUN make distclean && make -j"$(nproc)"
+
+# Use the revision as the package version for the time being
+ENV PKG_NAME libnvidia-container
+ENV PKG_VERS ${REVISION}
+ENV DIST_DIR=/tmp/${PKG_NAME}-${PKG_VERS}
+RUN mkdir -p $DIST_DIR /dist
+CMD make dist && \
+    make rpm && \
+    mv /tmp/${PKG_NAME}-${PKG_VERS}/*.rpm /dist;
diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index 41e8275c..bbdefaf8 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -33,10 +33,10 @@ REVISION 	 ?= $(shell git rev-parse HEAD)
 
 # Supported OSs by architecture
 AMD64_TARGETS := ubuntu20.04 ubuntu18.04 ubuntu16.04 debian10 debian9
-X86_64_TARGETS := centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8 amazonlinux2 opensuse-leap15.1
+X86_64_TARGETS := fedora35 centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8 amazonlinux2 opensuse-leap15.1
 PPC64LE_TARGETS := ubuntu18.04 ubuntu16.04 centos7 centos8 rhel7 rhel8
 ARM64_TARGETS := ubuntu18.04
-AARCH64_TARGETS := centos8 rhel8 amazonlinux2
+AARCH64_TARGETS := fedora35 centos8 rhel8 amazonlinux2
 
 # Define top-level build targets
 docker%: SHELL:=/bin/bash
@@ -132,6 +132,11 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --centos8%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
 --centos8%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
 
+# private fedora target with overrides
+--fedora%: OS := fedora
+--fedora%: CFLAGS := -I/usr/include/tirpc
+--fedora%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
+
 # private opensuse-leap target with overrides
 --opensuse-leap%: OS := opensuse-leap
 --opensuse-leap%: BASEIMAGE = opensuse/leap:$(VERSION)

author: Simon Fels
message: Preload libgcc_s.so.1 on arm64 systems
patch:
commit 18239b2aecec79ea35b6b1c4cbc7e2761b2a35b7
Author: Simon Fels <simon.fels@canonical.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 22 17:20:00 2022 +0200

    Preload libgcc_s.so.1 on arm64 systems
    
    The arm64 binary of libnvidia-ml.so.1 is linked against libgcc_s.so.1
    whereas the x86_64 binary is not. Due to this, when nvidia-container-cli
    is invoked with a rootfs path different than / (as in the case of the
    LXD snap) we will end up failing to setup the container environment with
    the following error:
    
    lxc c0 20220622134550.154 DEBUG    conf - conf.c:run_buffer:310 - Script exec
      /snap/lxd/current/lxc/hooks/nvidia produced output: nvidia-container-cli.real:
    lxc c0 20220622134550.154 DEBUG    conf - conf.c:run_buffer:310 - Script exec
      /snap/lxd/current/lxc/hooks/nvidia produced output: initialization error:
        load library failed: /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: version `glibc_2.35'
          not found (required by /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libgcc_s.so.1)
    
    This does not occur for other container manager (e.g. docker) as they
    typically run with the rootfs they come from being the host one. In case
    of the LXD snap that is not the case. Snaps use a base snap to define
    their rootfs. This give us the situation with the LXD snap from
    5.0/stable using the core20 base snap where LXD is running against an
    Ubuntu 20.04 rootfs (from the core20 snap) on an Ubuntu 22.04 host. The
    NVIDIA driver here is installed on the 22.04 host. nvidia-container-cli
    sits within the LXD snap and will chroot to the host. If libgcc_s.so.1
    is not preloaded, it will now fail with the error above.
    
    Preloading libgcc_s.so.1 as it's done for other dependencies already
    fixes the problem.
    
    See https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/nvidia-graphics-drivers-510/+bug/1971978
    for more details.
    
    Signed-off-by: Simon Fels <simon.fels@canonical.com>
---
 src/driver.c | 8 ++++++++
 1 file changed, 8 insertions(+)

diff --git a/src/driver.c b/src/driver.c
index a512361d..706f141a 100644
--- a/src/driver.c
+++ b/src/driver.c
@@ -128,6 +128,14 @@ driver_init_1_svc(ptr_t ctxptr, driver_init_res *res, maybe_unused struct svc_re
                         goto fail;
                 if (xdlopen(err, "libpthread.so.0", RTLD_NOW) == NULL)
                         goto fail;
+#if defined(__aarch64__)
+                /* libnvidia-ml.so.1 depends on libgcc_s.so.1 in its arm64 build. Not
+                 * preloading here will cause unresolved symbols when chrooting into the
+                 * container environment
+                 */
+                if (xdlopen(err, "libgcc_s.so.1", RTLD_NOW) == NULL)
+                        goto fail;
+#endif
 
                 if (chroot(ctx->root) < 0 || chdir("/") < 0) {
                         error_set(err, "change root failed");

author: Evan Lezar
message: Fix bug when running host ldconfig using glibc compiled with a non-standard prefix
patch:
commit f1de053be11318817e44ef2011c4f5d0a35bab70
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed May 11 07:17:58 2022 +0200

    Fix bug when running host ldconfig using glibc compiled with a non-standard prefix
    
    This change explicitly sets the ldcache and config paths when running ldconfig
    to the defaults of "/etc/ld.so.cache" and "/etc/ld.so.conf". This handles the fact that
    the host ldconfig may be expecting another path due to glibc being
    compiled with a prefix.
    
    See https://gitlab.com/nvidia/container-toolkit/libnvidia-container/-/issues/1
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 3 ++-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 3 ++-
 src/nvc_ldcache.c                      | 2 +-
 3 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 7af36f87..a12c8b58 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,8 +1,9 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.2-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
   * Bump libtirpc to 1.3.2
+  * Fix bug when running host ldconfig using glibc compiled with a non-standard prefix
 
- -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Tue, 10 May 2022 16:04:54 +0200
+ -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Wed, 11 May 2022 07:21:08 +0200
 
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index b4c849e0..cfbc2d39 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -99,8 +99,9 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 %{_bindir}/*
 
 %changelog
-* Tue May 10 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.2
+* Wed May 11 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.2
 - Bump libtirpc to 1.3.2
+- Fix bug when running host ldconfig using glibc compiled with a non-standard prefix
 
 * Thu Mar 24 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.1
 - [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dxg missing)
diff --git a/src/nvc_ldcache.c b/src/nvc_ldcache.c
index da8b0930..49589fa8 100644
--- a/src/nvc_ldcache.c
+++ b/src/nvc_ldcache.c
@@ -359,7 +359,7 @@ nvc_ldcache_update(struct nvc_context *ctx, const struct nvc_container *cnt)
         if (validate_args(ctx, cnt != NULL) < 0)
                 return (-1);
 
-        argv = (char * []){cnt->cfg.ldconfig, cnt->cfg.libs_dir, cnt->cfg.libs32_dir, NULL};
+        argv = (char * []){cnt->cfg.ldconfig, "-f", "/etc/ld.so.conf", "-C", "/etc/ld.so.cache", cnt->cfg.libs_dir, cnt->cfg.libs32_dir, NULL};
         if (*argv[0] == '@') {
                 /*
                  * We treat this path specially to be relative to the host filesystem.

author: Evan Lezar
message: Exit with error if dxcore initializes with no adapters found
patch:
commit bbb95a8a0d852abc7112d9fc251a3e4231d78f22
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 30 07:33:20 2022 +0200

    Exit with error if dxcore initializes with no adapters found
    
    If dxcore is considered initialized with no adapters found, this could lead to
    invalid accesses of adapter data structures later. This changes ensures that if
    no adapters are enumerated, the program terminates with an error instead of a
    segfault.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 1 +
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 1 +
 src/dxcore.c                           | 1 -
 src/nvc.c                              | 3 +++
 4 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index ac3bf5b9..6994e8b7 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
+  * [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dxg missing)
   * Ignore pending MIG mode when checking if a device is MIG enabled
   * [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dxg is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index 1b341fd6..b643e414 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 
 %changelog
 * Thu Mar 24 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.1
+- [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dxg missing)
 - Ignore pending MIG mode when checking if a device is MIG enabled
 - [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dxg is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
 
diff --git a/src/dxcore.c b/src/dxcore.c
index 551f9d2f..5a17ce1a 100644
--- a/src/dxcore.c
+++ b/src/dxcore.c
@@ -322,7 +322,6 @@ int dxcore_init_context(struct dxcore_context* pCtx)
 
         dxcore_enum_adapters(pCtx, &lib);
 
-
         log_info("dxcore layer initialized successfully");
         pCtx->initialized = 1;
 
diff --git a/src/nvc.c b/src/nvc.c
index 84e893b6..94117fc7 100644
--- a/src/nvc.c
+++ b/src/nvc.c
@@ -394,6 +394,9 @@ nvc_init(struct nvc_context *ctx, const struct nvc_config *cfg, const char *opts
         if (dxcore_init_context(&ctx->dxcore) < 0) {
                 log_info("dxcore initialization failed, continuing assuming a non-WSL environment");
                 ctx->dxcore.initialized = 0;
+        } else if (ctx->dxcore.adapterCount == 0) {
+                log_err("dxcore initialization succeeded but no adapters were found");
+                goto fail;
         }
 
         if (flags & OPT_LOAD_KMODS) {

author: Evan Lezar
message: Ignore pending MIG mode when checking whether MIG is enabled
patch:
commit e55c2a9e82f556f73bc55b37568dbde92ed4b3cf
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 25 13:39:17 2022 +0200

    Ignore pending MIG mode when checking whether MIG is enabled
    
    Since the pending MIG mode is only applied on restart, it can be ignored
    when checking whether an active device is MIG enabled.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 3 ++-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 1 +
 src/driver.c                           | 5 ++---
 3 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index eb6e682c..d8deb6da 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,9 +1,10 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
+  * Ignore pending MIG mode when checking if a device is MIG enabled
   * [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dxg is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
   * [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dxg missing)
 
- -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Thu, 24 Mar 2022 11:25:07 +0200
+ -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Thu, 24 Mar 2022 11:25:07 +0200
 
 libnvidia-container (1.9.0-1) UNRELEASED; urgency=medium
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index 5cc96af4..48b3ab73 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 
 %changelog
 * Thu Mar 24 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.1
+- Ignore pending MIG mode when checking if a device is MIG enabled
 - [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dxg is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
 - [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dxg missing)
 
diff --git a/src/driver.c b/src/driver.c
index 9579ec89..a512361d 100644
--- a/src/driver.c
+++ b/src/driver.c
@@ -643,7 +643,7 @@ driver_get_device_mig_enabled(struct error *err, struct driver_device *dev, bool
         // Initialize local variables.
         struct driver *ctx = driver_get_context();
         struct driver_get_device_mig_mode_res res = {0};
-        unsigned int current, pending;
+        unsigned int current;
         int rv = -1;
 
         // Initialize return values.
@@ -657,8 +657,7 @@ driver_get_device_mig_enabled(struct error *err, struct driver_device *dev, bool
                 // to determine if MIG mode is enabled or not.
                 case NVML_SUCCESS:
                         current = res.driver_get_device_mig_mode_res_u.mode.current;
-                        pending = res.driver_get_device_mig_mode_res_u.mode.pending;
-                        *enabled = (current == NVML_DEVICE_MIG_ENABLE) && (current == pending);
+                        *enabled = (current == NVML_DEVICE_MIG_ENABLE);
                         break;
                 // If the error indicates that the function wasn't found, then
                 // we are on an older version of NVML that doesn't support MIG.

         /* Device mounts */

author: Evan Lezar
message: Switch from centos:8 to centos:stream8 images to build centos8 packages
patch:
commit b1f852a978a4138d29dbf26b8927d14796494a9c
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 28 11:25:01 2022 +0200

    Switch from centos:8 to centos:stream8 images to build centos8 packages
    
    Due to the EOL of centos:8 we switch to centos:stream8 to build the centos8 and
    rhel8 packages.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/Dockerfile.centos | 10 ----------
 mk/docker.mk         |  2 ++
 2 files changed, 2 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mk/Dockerfile.centos b/mk/Dockerfile.centos
index b23478d0..ef871f0b 100644
--- a/mk/Dockerfile.centos
+++ b/mk/Dockerfile.centos
@@ -3,16 +3,6 @@ FROM ${BASEIMAGE}
 
 SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
 
-ARG BASEIMAGE
-# See https://www.centos.org/centos-linux-eol/
-# and https://stackoverflow.com/a/70930049 for move to vault.centos.org
-# and https://serverfault.com/questions/1093922/failing-to-run-yum-update-in-centos-8 for move to vault.epel.cloud
-RUN [[ "${BASEIMAGE}" != "centos:8" ]] || \
-    ( \
-      sed -i 's/mirrorlist/#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Linux-* && \
-      sed -i 's|#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.epel.cloud|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Linux-* \
-    )
-
 ARG OS_VERSION
 RUN if [ "${OS_VERSION}" = "8" ]; then \
         yum --enablerepo=powertools install -y \
diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index e3d1ff8b..41e8275c 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -130,6 +130,7 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --centos%: OS := centos
 --centos8%: CFLAGS := -I/usr/include/tirpc
 --centos8%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
+--centos8%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
 
 # private opensuse-leap target with overrides
 --opensuse-leap%: OS := opensuse-leap
@@ -141,6 +142,7 @@ docker-amd64-verify: $(patsubst %, %-verify, $(AMD64_TARGETS)) \
 --rhel%: ARTIFACTS_DIR = $(DIST_DIR)/rhel$(VERSION)/$(ARCH)
 --rhel8%: CFLAGS := -I/usr/include/tirpc
 --rhel8%: LDLIBS := -ltirpc
+--rhel8%: BASEIMAGE = quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
 
 --verify-rhel%: OS := centos
 --verify-rhel%: VERSION = $(patsubst rhel%-$(ARCH),%,$(TARGET_PLATFORM))

author: Evan Lezar
message: [CI] Add CI job for each distribuition-arch combination
patch:
commit 74ccb7178ab64f82e2a448eaa63982aedaed4f8c
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 24 16:04:00 2022 +0200

    [CI] Add CI job for each distribuition-arch combination
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 .gitlab-ci.yml | 347 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----------------
 1 file changed, 253 insertions(+), 94 deletions(-)

diff --git a/.gitlab-ci.yml b/.gitlab-ci.yml
index 88e0d357..6461caa6 100644
--- a/.gitlab-ci.yml
+++ b/.gitlab-ci.yml
@@ -1,114 +1,273 @@
-# Build packages for all supported OS / ARCH combinations
-
-stages:
-  - build-one
-  - build-all
-
-.build-setup: &build-setup
-  image: docker:19.03.8
+# Copyright (c) 2022, NVIDIA CORPORATION.  All rights reserved.
+#
+# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+# You may obtain a copy of the License at
+#
+#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+#
+# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+# limitations under the License.
 
+default:
+  image: docker:stable
   services:
-    - name: docker:19.03.8-dind
+    - name: docker:stable-dind
       command: ["--experimental"]
 
+stages:
+  - package-build
+
+# Define the package build helpers
+.multi-arch-build:
   before_script:
-  - apk update
-  - apk upgrade
-  - apk add coreutils build-base sed git bash make
-  - docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes -c yes
+    - apk add --no-cache coreutils build-base sed git bash make
+    - '[[ -n "${SKIP_QEMU_SETUP}" ]] || docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes -c yes'
 
-# build-one jobs build packages for a single OS / ARCH combination.
-#
-# They are run during the first stage of the pipeline as a smoke test to ensure
-# that we can successfully build packages on all of our architectures for a
-# single OS. They are triggered on any change to an MR. No artifacts are
-# produced as part of build-one jobs.
-.build-one-setup: &build-one-setup
-  <<: *build-setup
-  stage: build-one
-  only:
-    - merge_requests
-
-# build-all jobs build packages for every OS / ARCH combination we support.
-#
-# They are run under two conditions:
-# 1) Automatically whenever a new tag is pushed to the repo (e.g. v1.1.0)
-# 2) Manually by a reviewer just before merging a MR.
-#
-# Unlike build-one jobs, it takes a long time to build the full suite
-# OS / ARCH combinations, so this is optimized to only run once per MR
-# (assuming it all passes). A full set of artifacts including the packages
-# built for each OS / ARCH are produced as a result of these jobs.
-.build-all-setup: &build-all-setup
-  <<: *build-setup
-  stage: build-all
+.package-artifacts:
+  variables:
+    ARTIFACTS_NAME: "libnvidia-container-${CI_PIPELINE_ID}"
+    ARTIFACTS_ROOT: "libnvidia-container-${CI_PIPELINE_ID}"
+    DIST_DIR: ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/${ARTIFACTS_ROOT}
+
+.package-build:
+  extends:
+    - .multi-arch-build
+    - .package-artifacts
+  stage: package-build
   timeout: 2h 30m
-  rules:
-    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG
-      when: always
-    - if: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_ID
-      when: manual
-
-  variables:
-    ARTIFACTS_NAME: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-${CI_JOB_NAME}-artifacts-${CI_PIPELINE_ID}"
-    ARTIFACTS_DIR: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-artifacts-${CI_PIPELINE_ID}"
-    DIST_DIR: "${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/${ARTIFACTS_DIR}"
-
-  artifacts:
-    name: ${ARTIFACTS_NAME}
-    paths:
-      - ${ARTIFACTS_DIR}
-
-# The full set of build-one jobs organizes to build
-# ubuntu18.04 in parallel on each of our supported ARCHs.
-build-one-amd64:
-  <<: *build-one-setup
   script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk ubuntu18.04-amd64
+    - make -f mk/docker.mk ${DIST}-${ARCH}
 
-build-one-ppc64le:
-  <<: *build-one-setup
-  script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk ubuntu18.04-ppc64le
 
-build-one-arm64:
-  <<: *build-one-setup
-  script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk ubuntu18.04-arm64
+# Define the distribution targets
+.dist-amazonlinux2:
+  variables:
+    DIST: amazonlinux2
 
-# The full set of build-all jobs organized to
-# have builds for each ARCH run in parallel.
-build-all-amd64:
-  <<: *build-all-setup
-  script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk docker-amd64
+.dist-centos7:
+  variables:
+    DIST: centos7
+
+.dist-centos8:
+  variables:
+    DIST: centos8
+
+.dist-debian10:
+  variables:
+    DIST: debian10
+
+.dist-debian9:
+  variables:
+    DIST: debian9
+
+.dist-opensuse-leap15.1:
+  variables:
+    DIST: opensuse-leap15.1
+
+.dist-rhel7:
+  variables:
+    DIST: rhel7
+
+.dist-rhel8:
+  variables:
+    DIST: rhel8
+
+.dist-ubuntu16.04:
+  variables:
+    DIST: ubuntu16.04
+
+.dist-ubuntu18.04:
+  variables:
+    DIST: ubuntu18.04
+
+.dist-ubuntu20.04:
+  variables:
+    DIST: ubuntu20.04
+
+# Define architecture targets
+.arch-aarch64:
+  variables:
+    ARCH: aarch64
+
+.arch-amd64:
+  variables:
+    ARCH: amd64
+
+.arch-arm64:
+  variables:
+    ARCH: arm64
+
+.arch-ppc64le:
+  variables:
+    ARCH: ppc64le
+
+.arch-x86_64:
+  variables:
+    ARCH: x86_64
+
+# Define the package build targets
+package-amazonlinux2-aarch64:
+  extends:
+    - .package-build
+    - .dist-amazonlinux2
+    - .arch-aarch64
   needs:
-    - build-one-amd64
+    - package-ubuntu18.04-arm64
 
-build-all-x86_64:
-  <<: *build-all-setup
-  script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk docker-x86_64
+package-amazonlinux2-x86_64:
+  extends:
+    - .package-build
+    - .dist-amazonlinux2
+    - .arch-x86_64
   needs:
-    - build-one-amd64
+    - package-ubuntu18.04-amd64
 
-build-all-ppc64le:
-  <<: *build-all-setup
-  script:
-    - make -f mk/docker.mk docker-ppc64le
+package-centos7-ppc64le:
+  extends:

author: Evan Lezar
message: Skip filtering devices based on capability on WSL2
patch:
commit 9408f52ccd1489064591998b07ef2a5b40f9f53e
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 24 15:24:59 2022 +0200

    Skip filtering devices based on capability on WSL2
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      |  1 +
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec |  1 +
 src/nvc_mount.c                        | 15 +++++++++------
 3 files changed, 11 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 6363c207..29204e1f 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
+  * [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dgx is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
   * [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dgx missing)
 
  -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Thu, 24 Mar 2022 11:25:07 +0200
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index fc2f3c5a..cac2cdc5 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 
 %changelog
 * Thu Mar 24 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.1
+- [WSL2] Fix bug where /dev/dgx is not mounted when NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES does not include "compute"
 - [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dgx missing)
 
 * Fri Mar 18 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.9.0-1
diff --git a/src/nvc_mount.c b/src/nvc_mount.c
index b933eb72..85ef9f52 100644
--- a/src/nvc_mount.c
+++ b/src/nvc_mount.c
@@ -803,12 +803,15 @@ nvc_driver_mount(struct nvc_context *ctx, const struct nvc_container *cnt, const
 
         /* Device mounts */
         for (size_t i = 0; i < info->ndevs; ++i) {
-                /* XXX Only compute libraries require specific devices (e.g. UVM). */
-                if (!(cnt->flags & OPT_COMPUTE_LIBS) && major(info->devs[i].id) != NV_DEVICE_MAJOR)
-                        continue;
-                /* XXX Only display capability requires the modeset device. */
-                if (!(cnt->flags & OPT_DISPLAY) && minor(info->devs[i].id) == NV_MODESET_DEVICE_MINOR)
-                        continue;
+                /* On WSL2 we only mount the /dev/dgx device and as such these checks are not applicable. */
+                if (!ctx->dxcore.initialized) {
+                        /* XXX Only compute libraries require specific devices (e.g. UVM). */
+                        if (!(cnt->flags & OPT_COMPUTE_LIBS) && major(info->devs[i].id) != NV_DEVICE_MAJOR)
+                                continue;
+                        /* XXX Only display capability requires the modeset device. */
+                        if (!(cnt->flags & OPT_DISPLAY) && minor(info->devs[i].id) == NV_MODESET_DEVICE_MINOR)
+                                continue;
+                }
                 if (!(cnt->flags & OPT_NO_DEVBIND)) {
                         if ((*ptr++ = mount_device(&ctx->err, ctx->cfg.root, cnt, &info->devs[i])) == NULL)
                                 goto fail;

author: Evan Lezar
message: Log error and skip dxcore initialization if no adapters are found
patch:
commit 77449be6b468e19a115522150c03b5fb8d4f424d
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 24 11:20:09 2022 +0200

    Log error and skip dxcore initialization if no adapters are found
    
    If dxcore is considered initialized with no adapters found, this could lead to
    invalid accesses of adapter data structures later. This changes ensures that if
    no adapters are enumerated, the initialization of dxcore is not considered
    successful.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 2 +-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 2 +-
 src/dxcore.c                           | 4 ++++
 3 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 4d7ca3bc..6363c207 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.10.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
-  * dummy entry
+  * [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dgx missing)
 
  -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Thu, 24 Mar 2022 11:25:07 +0200
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index c5a32d7f..fc2f3c5a 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 
 %changelog
 * Thu Mar 24 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.10.0-0.1.rc.1
-- dummy entry
+- [WSL2] Fix segmentation fault on WSL2s system with no adpaters present (e.g. /dev/dgx missing)
 
 * Fri Mar 18 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.9.0-1
 - Add additional check for Tegra in /sys/.../family file in CLI
diff --git a/src/dxcore.c b/src/dxcore.c
index 551f9d2f..58dabdbf 100644
--- a/src/dxcore.c
+++ b/src/dxcore.c
@@ -322,6 +322,10 @@ int dxcore_init_context(struct dxcore_context* pCtx)
 
         dxcore_enum_adapters(pCtx, &lib);
 
+        if (pCtx->adapterCount == 0) {
+                log_err("dxcore library is present but no adapters were found");
+                goto error;
+        }
 
         log_info("dxcore layer initialized successfully");
         pCtx->initialized = 1;

author: Kevin Klues
message: Change default jetpack-specific CLI option for loading base CSV files
patch:
commit 6c46da1705151515b9dbe75be62d646eb07a1e5c
Author: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 21 16:18:09 2022 +0000

    Change default jetpack-specific CLI option for loading base CSV files
    
    Signed-off-by: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
---
 src/cli/configure.c | 15 +++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 15 insertions(+)

diff --git a/src/cli/configure.c b/src/cli/configure.c
index fdb400e0..415f6b38 100644
--- a/src/cli/configure.c
+++ b/src/cli/configure.c
@@ -68,7 +68,22 @@ configure_parser(int key, char *arg, struct argp_state *state)
                         goto fatal;
                 break;
         case 'r':
+                // Check for some special-purpose "requires" flags on tegra devices.
                 if (libnvc.version()->major == 0) {
+                        // If there is a special-purpose "requires" for
+                        // 'csv-mounts=all', then add the 'jetpack-mount-all'
+                        // container flag to tell the library to mount all CSV
+                        // files included in the jetpack release, instead of
+                        // just the base files.
+                        if (strncmp(arg, "csv-mounts=all", 14) == 0) {
+                                if (str_join(&err, &ctx->container_flags, "jetpack-mount-all", " ") < 0)
+                                        goto fatal;
+                                break;
+                        }
+                        // The 'base-only' setting is now the default for
+                        // tegra devices, but we need to keep this case here to
+                        // make sure we skip over it if encountered while
+                        // parsing the other "requires" arguments.
                         if (strncmp(arg, "base-only", 9) == 0) {
                                 if (str_join(&err, &ctx->container_flags, "jetpack-base-only", " ") < 0)
                                         goto fatal;

author: Evan Lezar
message: Fix logic to process --require flags on Jetson platforms
patch:
commit 56c30223e68e4f47a8ae7826d1b40c4afed459d5
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 4 10:25:04 2022 +0100

    Fix logic to process --require flags on Jetson platforms
    
    The current argument should only be skipped if it is "base-only". Further
    requirements should be processed the same on all platforms.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 3 ++-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 3 ++-
 src/cli/configure.c                    | 2 +-
 3 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index f2304803..145309c0 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,8 +1,9 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.9.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
+  * Process all --require flags on Jetson platforms
   * Fix long-standing issue with running ldconfig on Debian systems
 
- -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Fri, 18 Feb 2022 09:16:25 +0100
+ -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Mon, 21 Feb 2022 15:22:52 +0200
 
 libnvidia-container (1.8.1-1) UNRELEASED; urgency=medium
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index a9ff274d..cc11dac9 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -99,7 +99,8 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 %{_bindir}/*
 
 %changelog
-* Fri Feb 18 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.9.0-0.1.rc.1
+* Mon Feb 21 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.9.0-0.1.rc.1
+- Process all --require flags on Jetson platforms
 - Fix long-standing issue with running ldconfig on Debian systems
 
 * Mon Feb 14 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.8.1-1
diff --git a/src/cli/configure.c b/src/cli/configure.c
index ea33c24a..fdb400e0 100644
--- a/src/cli/configure.c
+++ b/src/cli/configure.c
@@ -72,8 +72,8 @@ configure_parser(int key, char *arg, struct argp_state *state)
                         if (strncmp(arg, "base-only", 9) == 0) {
                                 if (str_join(&err, &ctx->container_flags, "jetpack-base-only", " ") < 0)
                                         goto fatal;
+                                break;
                         }
-                        break;
                 }
                 if (ctx->nreqs >= nitems(ctx->reqs)) {
                         error_setx(&err, "too many requirements");

---

author: Kevin Klues
message: Fix centos8 build
patch:
commit 677179102b53abcdffd3c27fefd2112df09717d0
Author: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 18 08:51:09 2022 +0000

    Fix centos8 build
    
    Signed-off-by: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/Dockerfile.centos | 5 +++--
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mk/Dockerfile.centos b/mk/Dockerfile.centos
index ec12a414..b23478d0 100644
--- a/mk/Dockerfile.centos
+++ b/mk/Dockerfile.centos
@@ -5,11 +5,12 @@ SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
 
 ARG BASEIMAGE
 # See https://www.centos.org/centos-linux-eol/
-# and https://stackoverflow.com/a/70930049
+# and https://stackoverflow.com/a/70930049 for move to vault.centos.org
+# and https://serverfault.com/questions/1093922/failing-to-run-yum-update-in-centos-8 for move to vault.epel.cloud
 RUN [[ "${BASEIMAGE}" != "centos:8" ]] || \
     ( \
       sed -i 's/mirrorlist/#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Linux-* && \
-      sed -i 's|#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Linux-* \
+      sed -i 's|#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.epel.cloud|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-Linux-* \
     )
 
 ARG OS_VERSION

message: Specify docker platform args for build and run commands
patch:
commit fcfd96fdc1176117dc8162e70a1c407e3c793852
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 1 12:05:58 2022 +0100

    Specify docker platform args for build and run commands
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 mk/docker.mk | 2 ++
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)

diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk
index 0acc2e35..0e3b3426 100644
--- a/mk/docker.mk
+++ b/mk/docker.mk
@@ -150,6 +150,7 @@ docker-build-%: $(ARTIFACTS_DIR)
 	$(DOCKER) pull --platform=linux/$(ARCH) $(BASEIMAGE)
 	DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 \
 	$(DOCKER) build \
+	    --platform=linux/$(ARCH) \
 	    --progress=plain \
 	    --build-arg BASEIMAGE="$(BASEIMAGE)" \
 	    --build-arg OS_VERSION="$(VERSION)" \
@@ -167,6 +168,7 @@ docker-build-%: $(ARTIFACTS_DIR)
 	    --tag $(BUILDIMAGE) \
 	    --file $(DOCKERFILE) .
 	$(DOCKER) run \
+	    --platform=linux/$(ARCH) \
 	    -e TAG \
 	    -v $(ARTIFACTS_DIR):/dist \
 	    $(BUILDIMAGE)

 SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]

+RUN [[ "${BASEIMAGE}" != "centos:8" ]] || \

+    )
+

 RUN if [ "${OS_VERSION}" = "8" ]; then \
         yum --enablerepo=powertools install -y \

patch:

Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 27 12:47:51 2022 +0100

    Include libnvidia-pkcs11.so in compute libraries
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 3 ++-
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 3 ++-
 src/nvc_info.c                         | 1 +
 3 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 1d59b04a..d1f838ff 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,8 +1,9 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.8.0~rc.2-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
+  * Include libnvidia-pkcs11.so in compute libraries
   * Correct GSP firmware mount permissions
 
- -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Tue, 18 Jan 2022 11:20:38 +0100
+ -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> Thu, 27 Jan 2022 11:20:38 +0100
 
 libnvidia-container (1.8.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index 63b0333f..76ffd8ce 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -99,7 +99,8 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 %{_bindir}/*
 
 %changelog
-* Tue Jan 18 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.8.0-0.1.rc.2
+* Thu Jan 27 2022 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.8.0-0.1.rc.2
+- Include libnvidia-pkcs11.so in compute libraries
 - Correct GSP firmware mount permissions
 
 * Wed Dec 08 2021 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.8.0-0.1.rc.1
diff --git a/src/nvc_info.c b/src/nvc_info.c
index bad26866..4ce737d0 100644
--- a/src/nvc_info.c
+++ b/src/nvc_info.c
@@ -86,6 +86,7 @@ static const char * const compute_libs[] = {
         "libnvidia-fatbinaryloader.so",     /* fatbin loader (used by libcuda) */
         "libnvidia-allocator.so",           /* NVIDIA allocator runtime library */
         "libnvidia-compiler.so",            /* NVVM-PTX compiler for OpenCL (used by libnvidia-opencl) */
+        "libnvidia-pkcs11.so",              /* Encrypt/Decrypt library */
 };
 
 static const char * const video_libs[] = {

author: Kevin Klues
message: Support writing entire device filter eBPF program (if none present)

author: Kevin Klues
message: Remove custom code for getting existing eBPF program for device filters
patch:
commit c6a68b1eba9fedae22e042d54b01e5b226b6fab6
Author: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 22 06:10:58 2022 +0000

    Remove custom code for getting existing eBPF program for device filters
    
    Signed-off-by: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
---
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/prog.go      |  73 -------------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/prog_test.go | 168 ------------------------------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/ptr.go       |  38 -------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/ptr_32_be.go |  34 ------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/ptr_32_le.go |  34 ------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/ptr_64.go    |  33 ------
 src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/syscall.go   | 193 -----------------------------------
 7 files changed, 573 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/prog.go b/src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/prog.go
deleted file mode 100644
index 7db4f97b..00000000
--- a/src/nvcgo/internal/ebpf/prog.go
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,73 +0,0 @@
-/*
- * Copyright (c) 2021, NVIDIA CORPORATION.  All rights reserved.
- *
- * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
- * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
- * You may obtain a copy of the License at
- *
- *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
- *
- * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
- * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
- * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
- * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
- * limitations under the License.
- */
-
-package ebpf
-
-import (
-	"bytes"
-	"encoding/binary"
-	"io"
-	"unsafe"
-
-	"github.com/cilium/ebpf"
-	"github.com/cilium/ebpf/asm"
-)
-
-const (
-	bpfProgramLicense = "Apache"
-)
-
-type Program struct {
-	*ebpf.Program
-}
-
-var nativeByteOrder binary.ByteOrder
-
-func init() {
-	buf := [2]byte{}
-	*(*uint16)(unsafe.Pointer(&buf[0])) = uint16(0x00FF)
-
-	switch buf {
-	case [2]byte{0xFF, 0x00}:
-		nativeByteOrder = binary.LittleEndian
-	case [2]byte{0x00, 0xFF}:
-		nativeByteOrder = binary.BigEndian
-	default:
-		panic("Unable to infer byte order")
-	}
-}
-
-// GetInstructions gets the asm instructions associated with a given ebpf program
-func (prog *Program) GetInstructions() (asm.Instructions, string, error) {
-	info, err := getProgramInfoLinear(prog)
-	if err != nil {
-		return nil, "", err
-	}
-	reader := bytes.NewReader(info.Xlated_prog_insns)
-
-	var insts asm.Instructions
-	for {
-		var ins asm.Instruction
-		_, err := ins.Unmarshal(reader, nativeByteOrder)
-		if err == io.EOF {
-			return insts, bpfProgramLicense, nil
-		}
-		if err != nil {
-			return nil, "", err
-		}
-		insts = append(insts, ins)
-	}
-}

-		{

-			Type:        devices.CharDevice,
-			Major:       1,
-			Minor:       3,
-			Permissions: "rwm",
-		},
-		{
-			Allow:       true,

-			Minor:       9,
-			Permissions: "rwm",

-			Allow:       true,

-			Major:       5,
-			Minor:       0,
-			Permissions: "rwm",
-		},
-		{
-			Allow:       true,
-			Type:        devices.CharDevice,
-			Major:       5,
-			Minor:       1,
-			Permissions: "rwm",
-		},
-		{
-			Allow:       true,
-			Type:        devices.CharDevice,
-			Major:       5,
-			Minor:       2,
-			Permissions: "rwm",
-		},
-		{
-			Allow:       false,
-			Type:        devices.CharDevice,
-			Major:       10,
-			Minor:       229,
-			Permissions: "rwm",
-		},
-		{
-			Allow:       true,

-
-// NewPointer creates a 64-bit pointer from an unsafe Pointer.
-func NewPointer(ptr unsafe.Pointer) Pointer {
-	return Pointer{ptr: ptr}
-}
-
-// NewSlicePointer creates a 64-bit pointer from a byte slice.
-func NewSlicePointer(buf []byte) Pointer {
-	if len(buf) == 0 {
-		return Pointer{}
-	}
-
-	return Pointer{ptr: unsafe.Pointer(&buf[0])}
-}

-	Prog_tags                []byte

-	Run_cnt                  uint64

-
-type bpfObjGetInfoByFDAttr struct {
-	fd      uint32
-	infoLen uint32
-	info    Pointer
-}
-
-// getProgramInfoLinear returns a "linear" version of the progInfo struct with all array fields filled
-func getProgramInfoLinear(prog *Program) (*progInfoLinear, error) {
-	var info progInfo
-	attr := bpfObjGetInfoByFDAttr{
-		fd:      uint32(prog.FD()),
-		infoLen: uint32(unsafe.Sizeof(info)),
-		info:    NewPointer(unsafe.Pointer(&info)),
-	}
-
-	_, _, errno := unix.Syscall(unix.SYS_BPF, unix.BPF_OBJ_GET_INFO_BY_FD, uintptr(unsafe.Pointer(&attr)), unsafe.Sizeof(attr))
-	if errno != 0 {
-		return nil, fmt.Errorf("syscall SYS_BPF:BPF_OBJ_GET_INFO_BY_FD:attr %v: %v", attr, errno)
-	}
-
-	var infoLinear progInfoLinear
-	if info.Jited_prog_len > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Jited_prog_insns = make([]byte, info.Jited_prog_len)
-		info.Jited_prog_insns = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Jited_prog_insns)
-	}
-	if info.Xlated_prog_len > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Xlated_prog_insns = make([]byte, info.Xlated_prog_len)
-		info.Xlated_prog_insns = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Xlated_prog_insns)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_map_ids > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Map_ids = make([]byte, info.Nr_map_ids)
-		info.Map_ids = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Map_ids)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_jited_ksyms > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Jited_ksyms = make([]byte, info.Nr_jited_ksyms)
-		info.Jited_ksyms = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Jited_ksyms)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_jited_func_lens > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Jited_func_lens = make([]byte, info.Nr_jited_func_lens)
-		info.Jited_func_lens = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Jited_func_lens)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_func_info > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Func_info = make([]byte, info.Nr_func_info)
-		info.Func_info = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Func_info)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_line_info > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Line_info = make([]byte, info.Nr_line_info)
-		info.Line_info = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Line_info)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_jited_line_info > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Jited_line_info = make([]byte, info.Nr_jited_line_info)
-		info.Jited_line_info = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Jited_line_info)
-	}
-	if info.Nr_prog_tags > 0 {
-		infoLinear.Prog_tags = make([]byte, info.Nr_prog_tags)
-		info.Prog_tags = NewSlicePointer(infoLinear.Prog_tags)
-	}
-
-	_, _, errno = unix.Syscall(unix.SYS_BPF, unix.BPF_OBJ_GET_INFO_BY_FD, uintptr(unsafe.Pointer(&attr)), unsafe.Sizeof(attr))
-	if errno != 0 {
-		return nil, fmt.Errorf("syscall SYS_BPF:BPF_OBJ_GET_INFO_BY_FD:attr %v: %v", attr, errno)
-	}
-
-	infoLinear.Prog_type = info.Prog_type
-	infoLinear.Id = info.Id
-	infoLinear.Tag = info.Tag
-	infoLinear.Jited_prog_len = info.Jited_prog_len
-	infoLinear.Xlated_prog_len = info.Xlated_prog_len
-	infoLinear.Load_time = info.Load_time
-	infoLinear.Created_by_uid = info.Created_by_uid
-	infoLinear.Nr_map_ids = info.Nr_map_ids
-	infoLinear.Name = info.Name
-	infoLinear.Ifindex = info.Ifindex
-	infoLinear.Gpl_compatible = info.Gpl_compatible
-	infoLinear.Netns_dev = info.Netns_dev
-	infoLinear.Netns_ino = info.Netns_ino
-	infoLinear.Nr_jited_ksyms = info.Nr_jited_ksyms
-	infoLinear.Nr_jited_func_lens = info.Nr_jited_func_lens
-	infoLinear.Btf_id = info.Btf_id
-	infoLinear.Func_info_rec_size = info.Func_info_rec_size
-	infoLinear.Nr_func_info = info.Nr_func_info
-	infoLinear.Nr_line_info = info.Nr_line_info
-	infoLinear.Nr_jited_line_info = info.Nr_jited_line_info
-	infoLinear.Line_info_rec_size = info.Line_info_rec_size
-	infoLinear.Jited_line_info_rec_size = info.Jited_line_info_rec_size
-	infoLinear.Nr_prog_tags = info.Nr_prog_tags
-	infoLinear.Run_time_ns = info.Run_time_ns

-
-	return &infoLinear, nil
-}

author: Kevin Klues
message: Update changelog for 1.8.0-rc.1
patch:
commit a40148f23dd4e2c634df1f884685331430f30491
Author: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Dec 7 21:27:23 2021 +0000

    Update changelog for 1.8.0-rc.1
    
    Signed-off-by: Kevin Klues <kklues@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/changelog                      | 17 ++++++++++++++---
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec | 15 +++++++++++++--
 2 files changed, 27 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/changelog b/pkg/deb/changelog
index 4532a0c2..8364dddd 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/changelog
+++ b/pkg/deb/changelog
@@ -1,8 +1,19 @@
 libnvidia-container (1.8.0~rc.1-1) experimental; urgency=medium
 
-  * [placheholder for 1.8.0~rc.1-1]
-
- -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Fri, 03 Dec 2021 15:02:21 +0100
+  * Add new cgroup.c file based on nvcgo that supports both cgroupv1 and cgroupv2
+  * Move cgroup.c to cgroup_legacy.c in preparation for nvcgo implementation
+  * Install libnvidia-container-go.so from deps directory
+  * Fix DESTDIR for deps make target
+  * Create a go-shared library called nvcgo and wrap it in an RPC service
+  * Cleanup the nvc_shutdown() path when there is an error in RPC services
+  * Move from an nvc_context specific RPC 'driver' service to a global one
+  * Generalize RPC mechanism to be instantated multiple times
+  * Split RPC mechanism from 'driver' code into standalone RPC mechanism
+  * Add -fplan9-extensions to Makefile to allow structs to be "extended"
+  * Cleanup dead code in various components
+  * Allow build-all jobs to be triggered earlier for more parallelism in CI
+
+ -- NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com>  Wed, 08 Dec 2021 12:27:03 +0100
 
 libnvidia-container (1.7.0-1) UNRELEASED; urgency=medium
 
diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
index b3289744..dfbef466 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/libnvidia-container.spec
@@ -99,8 +99,19 @@ This package contains command-line tools that facilitate using the library.
 %{_bindir}/*
 
 %changelog
-* Fri Dec 03 2021 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.8.0-0.1.rc.1
-- [placheholder for 1.8.0~rc.1-1]

+- Add new cgroup.c file based on nvcgo that supports both cgroupv1 and cgroupv2
+- Move cgroup.c to cgroup_legacy.c in preparation for nvcgo implementation
+- Install libnvidia-container-go.so from deps directory
+- Fix DESTDIR for deps make target
+- Create a go-shared library called nvcgo and wrap it in an RPC service
+- Cleanup the nvc_shutdown() path when there is an error in RPC services
+- Move from an nvc_context specific RPC 'driver' service to a global one
+- Generalize RPC mechanism to be instantated multiple times
+- Split RPC mechanism from 'driver' code into standalone RPC mechanism
+- Add -fplan9-extensions to Makefile to allow structs to be "extended"
+- Cleanup dead code in various components
+- Allow build-all jobs to be triggered earlier for more parallelism in CI
 
 * Tue Nov 30 2021 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 1.7.0-1
 - Promote 1.7.0-0.1.rc.1 to 1.7.0-1

---
 mk/docker.mk | 5 ++++-
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/mk/docker.mk b/mk/docker.mk

author: Evan Lezar
message: Suppress lintian warnings regarding exported symbols
patch:
commit 93fc59e2f51fac44db5f19151824a184e02a635a
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Dec 7 13:34:39 2021 +0100

    Suppress lintian warnings regarding exported symbols
    
    Suppress a lintian error caused by including libnvidia-container-go
    in the packages and this being picked up by dh_makeshlibs:
    
    This could be addressed by moving to a shlibs policy instead of symbols.
    See  http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/doc/debian-policy/policy.html/ch-sharedlibs.html
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 pkg/deb/libnvidia-container@MAJOR@.lintian-overrides | 3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)

diff --git a/pkg/deb/libnvidia-container@MAJOR@.lintian-overrides b/pkg/deb/libnvidia-container@MAJOR@.lintian-overrides
index b5de7f67..eb66c84a 100644
--- a/pkg/deb/libnvidia-container@MAJOR@.lintian-overrides
+++ b/pkg/deb/libnvidia-container@MAJOR@.lintian-overrides
@@ -1 +1,4 @@
 new-package-should-close-itp-bug
+# We suppress the following lintian error due to including libnvidia-container-go in the packages and this being picked up by dh_makeshlibs:
+# E: libnvidia-container1: symbols-file-contains-current-version-with-debian-revision on symbol AddDeviceRules@Base and 46 others
+symbols-file-contains-current-version-with-debian-revision

author: Evan Lezar
message: Install libnvidia-container-go from deps
patch:
commit 2a3663f0328a05dcad8bce2c5dfab3a3fde8a2bd
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Dec 6 15:34:08 2021 +0100

    Install libnvidia-container-go from deps
    
    This change ensures that the libnvida-contianer-go shared library
    is inclded in the install target.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 Makefile           | 14 +++++++++++++-
 src/nvcgo/Makefile | 36 +++++++++++++++++++-----------------
 2 files changed, 32 insertions(+), 18 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 789ef6ca..80545798 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -131,6 +131,11 @@ LIB_SONAME  := $(LIB_NAME).so.$(MAJOR)
 LIB_SYMLINK := $(LIB_NAME).so
 LIB_PKGCFG  := $(LIB_NAME).pc
 
+LIBGO_NAME    := $(LIB_NAME)-go
+LIBGO_SHARED  := $(LIBGO_NAME).so.$(VERSION)
+LIBGO_SONAME  := $(LIBGO_NAME).so.$(MAJOR)
+LIBGO_SYMLINK := $(LIBGO_NAME).so
+
 ##### Flags definitions #####
 
 # Common flags
@@ -255,7 +260,7 @@ deps: $(LIB_RPC_SRCS) $(BUILD_DEFS)
 	$(MKDIR) -p $(DEPS_DIR)
 	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvidia-modprobe.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) install
 ifeq ($(WITH_NVCGO), yes)
-	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvcgo.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) VERSION=$(VERSION) install
+	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvcgo.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) MAJOR=$(MAJOR) VERSION=$(VERSION) LIB_NAME=$(LIBGO_NAME) install
 endif
 ifeq ($(WITH_LIBELF), no)
 	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/elftoolchain.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) install
@@ -272,6 +277,10 @@ install: all
 	$(INSTALL) -m 644 $(LIB_STATIC) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)
 	$(INSTALL) -m 755 $(LIB_SHARED) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)
 	$(LN) -sf $(LIB_SONAME) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)/$(LIB_SYMLINK)
+ifeq ($(WITH_NVCGO), yes)
+	$(INSTALL) -m 755 $(DEPS_DIR)$(libdir)/$(LIBGO_SHARED) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)
+	$(LN) -sf $(LIBGO_SONAME) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)/$(LIBGO_SYMLINK)
+endif
 	$(LDCONFIG) -n $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)
 	# Install debugging symbols
 	$(INSTALL) -m 644 $(DEBUG_DIR)/$(LIB_SONAME) $(DESTDIR)$(libdbgdir)
@@ -288,6 +297,9 @@ uninstall:
 	$(RM) $(addprefix $(DESTDIR)$(includedir)/,$(notdir $(LIB_INCS)))
 	# Uninstall library files
 	$(RM) $(addprefix $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)/,$(LIB_STATIC) $(LIB_SHARED) $(LIB_SONAME) $(LIB_SYMLINK))
+ifeq ($(WITH_NVCGO), yes)
+	$(RM) $(addprefix $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)/,$(LIBGO_SHARED) $(LIBGO_SONAME) $(LIBGO_SYMLINK))
+endif
 	# Uninstall debugging symbols
 	$(RM) $(DESTDIR)$(libdbgdir)/$(LIB_SONAME)
 	# Uninstall configuration files
diff --git a/src/nvcgo/Makefile b/src/nvcgo/Makefile
index 4cc4927b..eaee95a4 100644
--- a/src/nvcgo/Makefile
+++ b/src/nvcgo/Makefile
@@ -19,33 +19,35 @@ INSTALL ?= install
 
 DESTDIR ?=
 VERSION ?= devel
+MAJOR   ?= 1
 
-PKGNAME := nvcgo
-LIBNAME := libnvidia-container-go
-OBJNAME := $(LIBNAME).so
-HDRNAME := $(LIBNAME).h
-SONAME  := $(LIBNAME).so.1
-DSTNAME := $(LIBNAME).so.$(VERSION)
-CTYPESH := ctypes.h
+PKG_NAME := nvcgo
+
+LIB_NAME    ?= libnvidia-container-go
+LIB_SHARED  ?= $(LIB_NAME).so.$(VERSION)
+LIB_SONAME  ?= $(LIB_NAME).so.$(MAJOR)
+
+OBJ_NAME := $(LIB_NAME).so
+HDR_NAME := $(LIB_NAME).h
+CTYPES_H := ctypes.h
 
 CGO_CFLAGS  := -std=gnu11 -O2
-CGO_LDFLAGS := -Wl,--gc-sections -Wl,-s -Wl,-soname,$(SONAME)
+CGO_LDFLAGS := -Wl,--gc-sections -Wl,-s -Wl,-soname,$(LIB_SONAME)
 
-build: $(OBJNAME)
+build: $(OBJ_NAME)
 
-$(OBJNAME): $(wildcard $(CURDIR)/*.go) $(wildcard */*.go)
+$(OBJ_NAME): $(wildcard $(CURDIR)/*.go) $(wildcard */*.go)
 	export CGO_CFLAGS="$(CGO_CFLAGS)"; \
 	export CGO_LDFLAGS="$(CGO_LDFLAGS)"; \
 	$(GO) build -o $(@) -ldflags "-s -w" -buildmode=c-shared .
 
-install: $(OBJNAME)
-	$(MKDIR) -p $(DESTDIR)/usr/local/lib
-	$(MKDIR) -p $(DESTDIR)/usr/local/include/$(PKGNAME)
-	$(INSTALL) $(OBJNAME) $(DESTDIR)/usr/local/lib/$(DSTNAME)
-	$(INSTALL) $(HDRNAME) $(DESTDIR)/usr/local/include/$(PKGNAME)/$(PKGNAME).h
-	$(INSTALL) $(CTYPESH) $(DESTDIR)/usr/local/include/$(PKGNAME)/$(CTYPESH)
+install: $(OBJ_NAME)
+	$(INSTALL) -d -m 755 $(addprefix $(DESTDIR),$(libdir) $(includedir)/$(PKG_NAME))
+	$(INSTALL) -m 755 $(OBJ_NAME) $(DESTDIR)$(libdir)/$(LIB_SHARED)
+	$(INSTALL) -m 644 $(HDR_NAME) $(DESTDIR)$(includedir)/$(PKG_NAME)/$(PKG_NAME).h
+	$(INSTALL) -m 644 $(CTYPES_H) $(DESTDIR)$(includedir)/$(PKG_NAME)/$(CTYPES_H)
 
 clean:
-	$(RM) -f $(SONAME) $(HDRNAME)
+	$(RM) -f $(OBJ_NAME) $(HDR_NAME)
 
 .PHONY: build clean install

author: Evan Lezar
message: Fix DESTDIR for deps make target
patch:
commit bcaafec6fec8f281f88e1369720ce18759ef187e
Author: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Dec 7 11:17:38 2021 +0100

    Fix DESTDIR for deps make target
    
    DESTDIR is a builtin make variable and cannot be overridden in
    child make processes using export. This change explicitly sets
    DESTDIR for the deps make targets which invokes the install
    target for the dependencies.
    
    Signed-off-by: Evan Lezar <elezar@nvidia.com>
---
 Makefile | 9 ++++-----
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index c7bb00e9..789ef6ca 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -251,18 +251,17 @@ shared: $(LIB_SHARED)
 
 static: $(LIB_STATIC)($(LIB_STATIC_OBJ))
 
-deps: export DESTDIR:=$(DEPS_DIR)
 deps: $(LIB_RPC_SRCS) $(BUILD_DEFS)
 	$(MKDIR) -p $(DEPS_DIR)
-	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvidia-modprobe.mk install
+	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvidia-modprobe.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) install
 ifeq ($(WITH_NVCGO), yes)
-	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvcgo.mk VERSION=$(VERSION) install
+	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/nvcgo.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) VERSION=$(VERSION) install
 endif
 ifeq ($(WITH_LIBELF), no)
-	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/elftoolchain.mk install
+	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/elftoolchain.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) install
 endif
 ifeq ($(WITH_TIRPC), yes)
-	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/libtirpc.mk install
+	$(MAKE) -f $(MAKE_DIR)/libtirpc.mk DESTDIR=$(DEPS_DIR) install
 endif
 
 install: all
